title: parahebe muelleri
intro: Parahebe muelleri, also known as the green flower or New Zealand hebe, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It is used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in treating various health conditions. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating cold and flu symptoms, and promoting wound healing. Parahebe muelleri is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some cases, it is used in topical applications to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
